title: One Piece
synopsis: Monkey D. Luffy sets sail to find the legendary One Piece treasure and become King of the Pirates, gathering a crew of misfits along the way. A sprawling epic spanning oceans, continents, and secret histories.
reviews: The greatest long-running manga of all time.
Incredible world-building and emotional depth.

title: Naruto
synopsis: Naruto Uzumaki, a young ninja with a sealed demon fox inside him, dreams of becoming Hokage to earn his village's respect. His journey from outcast to hero shapes the fate of the ninja world.
reviews: A defining work of the shonen genre.
The early arcs are incredible.

title: Dragon Ball
synopsis: Son Goku travels the world collecting Dragon Balls and trains to become the strongest fighter in the universe. His adventures evolve from comedic martial arts to epic cosmic battles.
reviews: The blueprint for modern shonen.
Dragon Ball Z changed action manga forever.

title: Demon Slayer: Kimetsu no Yaiba
synopsis: Tanjiro Kamado becomes a demon slayer after demons massacre his family and turn his sister Nezuko into a demon. A beautifully drawn story of brotherhood, grief, and redemption set in Taisho-era Japan.
reviews: Gorgeous art and deeply moving.
The fight choreography is unmatched.

title: Attack on Titan
synopsis: In a world where humanity hides behind walls to survive giant humanoid Titans, Eren Yeager vows revenge after witnessing his mother's death. A complex political and philosophical saga that challenges notions of freedom and justice.
reviews: One of the most ambitious manga narratives ever written.
The ending is divisive but the journey is incredible.

title: Hunter x Hunter
synopsis: Gon Freecss pursues his missing father, the legendary Hunter, in a world full of supernatural abilities and dangerous beasts. The series subverts shonen conventions with deeply strategic battles and dark themes.
reviews: The Chimera Ant arc is a masterpiece.
Best power system in shonen.

title: Bleach
synopsis: Ichigo Kurosaki gains the powers of a Soul Reaper and must defend the living world from evil spirits called Hollows. A visually dynamic series featuring elaborate sword techniques and a vast supernatural cosmology.
reviews: Incredible early arcs and iconic character designs.
The Thousand-Year Blood War arc is stunning.

title: My Hero Academia
synopsis: In a world where almost everyone has a superpower called a Quirk, powerless Izuku Midoriya inherits the greatest ability and enrolls in a high school for heroes. A love letter to superhero comics filtered through shonen manga tradition.
reviews: Great character work and inventive Quirk design.
A worthy successor to classic shonen.

title: Fairy Tail
synopsis: Natsu Dragneel and his friends in the Fairy Tail wizards' guild go on magical adventures while protecting their bonds of friendship. A warm and bombastic fantasy series with an emphasis on camaraderie.
reviews: Incredibly fun and full of heart.
The guild dynamics are its greatest strength.

title: Black Clover
synopsis: Asta, born without magic in a magical world, aims to become the Magic Emperor through sheer willpower and anti-magic powers. A high-energy shonen that celebrates perseverance and hard work.
reviews: Exceeds its rough start to deliver great action.
The magic system is creative.

title: Jujutsu Kaisen
synopsis: Yuji Itadori swallows a cursed finger and becomes host to the King of Curses, joining a secret school that exorcises supernatural monsters. Dark, witty, and filled with some of the most inventive fight sequences in modern manga.
reviews: The best new-generation shonen.
Gege Akutami is a genius at action choreography.

title: Fullmetal Alchemist
synopsis: Brothers Edward and Alphonse Elric seek the Philosopher's Stone to restore their bodies after a failed attempt to resurrect their mother. A masterfully plotted story exploring sacrifice, redemption, and the ethics of alchemy.
reviews: The most perfectly structured manga ever written.
Every chapter matters. Nothing is wasted.

title: Soul Eater
synopsis: Students at the Death Weapon Meister Academy train with weapon-human partners to collect souls and prevent the resurrection of dark gods. A stylish supernatural action series with gothic aesthetics.
reviews: Incredible visual style and fun character dynamics.
The early arcs are some of the best in shonen.

title: Yu Yu Hakusho
synopsis: Delinquent teenager Yusuke Urameshi is resurrected as a Spirit Detective tasked with policing the spirit world. A classic tournament-style shonen that defined a generation of supernatural action manga.
reviews: The Dark Tournament is peak shonen.
Togashi's first masterpiece before HxH.

title: Inuyasha
synopsis: Modern schoolgirl Kagome is transported to feudal Japan, where she teams up with half-demon Inuyasha to collect the shards of a powerful jewel. A sweeping adventure blending historical fantasy and romance.
reviews: A beautiful blend of action and romance.
Rumiko Takahashi at her most epic.

title: D.Gray-man
synopsis: Allen Walker battles the Millennium Earl and his demonic Akuma with the holy weapon Anti-Akuma. A dark and emotionally complex story about the price of being chosen by God.
reviews: Deeply underrated dark shonen.
The lore is fascinating and the characters unforgettable.

title: Blue Exorcist
synopsis: Rin Okumura discovers he is the son of Satan and enrolls in True Cross Academy to become an exorcist. A compelling exploration of identity as Rin fights his demonic heritage while protecting the human world.
reviews: Great main character with a fascinating premise.
The school setting grounds the supernatural elements well.

title: Assassination Classroom
synopsis: A super-powered creature destroys most of the moon and threatens Earth, but offers humanity a chance if a class of misfits can assassinate him before graduation. Surprisingly heartfelt despite its absurd premise.
reviews: One of the most creative manga premises in years.
The ending made me cry.

title: Mob Psycho 100
synopsis: Shigeo Kageyama, an emotionally suppressed boy with enormous psychic powers, works for a con-man exorcist while trying to fit in at school. A deeply humanistic story about growing up and not letting your gifts define you.
reviews: ONE's best work, full of surprising emotional depth.
The art style is deceptively expressive.

title: One-Punch Man
synopsis: Saitama trained so hard he became so powerful that he can defeat any enemy with a single punch, leaving him perpetually bored. A brilliant parody of hero tropes that also celebrates heroism.
reviews: The funniest and most inventive superhero deconstruction in manga.
Murata's art on the remake is jaw-dropping.

title: Kimi ni Todoke
synopsis: Sawako Kuronuma, nicknamed after the horror film Sadako for her shy demeanor, falls for the class's most popular boy Kazehaya. A tender slow-burn romance about learning to open up and accept kindness.
reviews: The sweetest romance in manga.
Beautifully captures the anxiety of first love.

title: NANA
synopsis: Two young women named Nana meet on a train to Tokyo and become unlikely roommates as their lives intertwine around love and music. A raw and honest portrayal of female friendship and heartbreak.
reviews: Devastatingly real portrayal of young adult life.
Ai Yazawa's masterpiece.

title: Boys Over Flowers
synopsis: Tsukushi Makino clashes with the F4, the most powerful clique at her elite school, but falls for their leader Domyoji. A foundational shoujo romance with a feisty heroine and iconic class-divide tension.
reviews: The original school romance manga.
Domyoji is the template for every problematic love interest since.

title: Fruits Basket
synopsis: Orphaned Tohru Honda is taken in by the mysterious Sohma family, who transform into zodiac animals when hugged by the opposite sex. A gentle story about trauma, acceptance, and the power of unconditional love.
reviews: One of the most emotionally mature shojo manga.
The full edition is a masterpiece of storytelling.

title: Ouran High School Host Club
synopsis: Scholarship student Haruhi Fujioka accidentally breaks a vase and must cross-dress as a host to pay off the debt. A witty and self-aware reverse-harem that lovingly parodies its own genre.
reviews: The smartest and funniest shoujo manga.
Haruhi is one of the best heroines in the genre.

title: Sailor Moon
synopsis: Clumsy teenager Usagi Tsukino becomes Sailor Moon, a magical warrior destined to protect the Earth alongside her Sailor Senshi. A cornerstone of the magical girl genre with enduring themes of love and girl power.
reviews: The defining magical girl manga.
Naoko Takeuchi created a template that still defines the genre.

title: Cardcaptor Sakura
synopsis: Young Sakura Kinomoto accidentally releases magical Clow Cards into the world and must capture them all before disaster strikes. A delightful magical girl adventure with beautifully designed outfits and a gentle romance.
reviews: CLAMP's most beloved work.
The most charming manga ever made.

title: Skip Beat!
synopsis: Kyoko Mogami enters showbiz for revenge on the boy who broke her heart, but discovers her own passion for acting along the way. A clever and emotionally dynamic story about ambition and self-discovery.
reviews: The best long-running shoujo manga of its era.
Kyoko's character development is extraordinary.

title: Ao Haru Ride
synopsis: Futaba Yoshioka reunites with her first love Kou in high school, only to find he has become a completely different person. A nuanced exploration of how childhood wounds shape our adult relationships.
reviews: The most emotionally honest school romance manga.
Beautifully drawn with incredible character nuance.

title: Monthly Girls' Nozaki-kun
synopsis: High schooler Chiyo confesses her feelings to Nozaki only to be recruited as his manga assistant, as he is secretly a popular girls' manga artist. A sharp and hilarious meta-comedy about romance manga tropes.
reviews: The funniest shoujo manga.
Every chapter has at least one perfect joke.

title: Berserk
synopsis: The lone mercenary Guts battles through a brutal medieval world haunted by demons while seeking revenge on his former commander Griffith. A dark masterpiece of graphic violence and profound philosophical depth.
reviews: One of the greatest manga ever created.
Kentaro Miura left behind an unfinished monument.

title: Nausicaä of the Valley of the Wind
synopsis: In a post-apocalyptic world covered by toxic forests, Princess Nausicaä seeks to understand the relationship between humanity and nature. Miyazaki's pre-Studio Ghibli epic is a visionary work of ecological science fiction.
reviews: A masterpiece of ecological storytelling.
Miyazaki's magnum opus.

title: Delicious in Dungeon
synopsis: When adventurer Laios's party runs out of food deep in a dungeon, they decide to cook and eat the monsters they defeat. A clever and heartwarming fantasy that uses food as a lens for exploring its rich world.
reviews: The most creative fantasy manga in years.
The world-building through food is genius.

title: Claymore
synopsis: Half-human, half-monster warriors called Claymores protect humanity from demonic Yoma in a medieval world. A grim and beautifully drawn action series about sacrifice and the monster within.
reviews: Criminally underrated dark fantasy.
Clare's character development is exceptional.

title: Mushishi
synopsis: Ginko travels through a timeless world as a Mushi Master, studying strange life forms called Mushi that interact with human lives in uncanny ways. A contemplative and haunting anthology series unlike any other.
reviews: The most peaceful and haunting manga ever written.
Every story is a perfect short film.

title: Natsume's Book of Friends
synopsis: Natsume Takashi can see supernatural spirits and inherits his grandmother's Book of Friends, a registry of spirit names she captured. A gentle and melancholic series about loneliness, belonging, and the bonds between worlds.
reviews: The most emotionally comforting manga.
Each arc is a small gem of storytelling.

title: The Rising of the Shield Hero
synopsis: Naofumi Iwatani is summoned to a fantasy world as the Shield Hero, but is immediately falsely accused and must rebuild from nothing. A dark isekai narrative about betrayal, survival, and reclaiming one's dignity.
reviews: A compelling dark isekai with a compelling protagonist.
More emotionally complex than most in its genre.

title: Made in Abyss
synopsis: Young orphan Riko descends into a mysterious chasm called the Abyss to find her missing mother, guided by a mysterious robot boy. Combines gorgeous art and childlike wonder with increasingly brutal horror.
reviews: The most beautiful and harrowing manga.
You will never feel the same way about adventure after this.

title: Overlord
synopsis: Gamer Momonga is trapped in a fantasy game world as his all-powerful undead character and decides to dominate this new realm. An interesting isekai told from the perspective of an overpowered villain protagonist.
reviews: The most interesting take on the OP protagonist isekai.
Great secondary cast.

title: Re:Zero
synopsis: Subaru Natsuki is transported to a fantasy world with a mysterious ability to return from death, reliving loops of traumatic experiences. A psychologically intense isekai about perseverance in the face of despair.
reviews: The most psychologically complex isekai.
The suffering is real but so is the growth.

title: Ghost in the Shell
synopsis: Cyborg police operative Major Motoko Kusanagi investigates cybercrime in a near-future Japan where the boundary between human and machine is blurred. A foundational cyberpunk work that interrogates identity and consciousness.
reviews: The most philosophically rich sci-fi manga.
Still feels relevant and prescient decades later.

title: Akira
synopsis: In neo-Tokyo, biker gang leader Kaneda tries to save his friend Tetsuo after government experiments unleash unstoppable psychic powers. A landmark of manga and anime that defined cyberpunk aesthetics worldwide.
reviews: The most visually spectacular manga.
Otomo's masterwork is still unmatched in scope.

title: Neon Genesis Evangelion
synopsis: Teenager Shinji Ikari pilots a giant biomechanical robot called an Evangelion to fight apocalyptic beings called Angels. A deconstruction of mecha genre conventions wrapped in dense psychological introspection.
reviews: Changed how anime and manga approach character psychology.
Still the definitive mecha deconstruction.

title: Planetes
synopsis: A team of space debris collectors in the mid-21st century navigate their personal dreams against the mundane realities of life in orbit. A grounded and humanistic space opera about work and aspiration.
reviews: The most adult and grounded sci-fi manga.
Yukimura writes humans with extraordinary empathy.

title: 20th Century Boys
synopsis: Kenji and his childhood friends realize the cult threatening the world is acting out a story they invented as children. A sprawling thriller that weaves childhood nostalgia with global apocalyptic conspiracy.
reviews: Urasawa's most ambitious epic.
An intricate puzzle box that pays off magnificently.

title: Biomega
synopsis: Synthetic human Zouichi and his AI bear companion Cibo traverse a post-apocalyptic wasteland overrun by mutating zombie drones. A relentless, visually dense sci-fi action manga from the creator of Blame!
reviews: Nihei's most accessible work.
Pure dystopian action poetry.

title: Terra Formars
synopsis: Centuries after cockroaches are sent to terraform Mars, they have evolved into humanoid killers, and heavily modified humans are sent to exterminate them. A brutal sci-fi action series with extreme body horror.
reviews: Wildly inventive sci-fi with incredible creature design.
Not for the faint-hearted.

title: BLAME!
synopsis: The lone wanderer Killy travels through an incomprehensibly vast cyberpunk mega-structure searching for humans with the Net Terminal Gene. A minimalist, atmospheric manga told almost entirely through stunning visuals.
reviews: Unlike anything else in manga.
Nihei creates atmosphere purely through architecture.

title: Gantz
synopsis: After dying in a train accident, Kei Kurono is revived and forced to hunt down aliens by a mysterious black sphere called Gantz. An ultra-violent deconstruction of the isekai concept exploring mortality and human nature.
reviews: Brutally honest about fear and survival instinct.
The alien designs are extraordinary.

title: Dr. Stone
synopsis: Genius Senku Ishigami awakens 3,700 years after a mysterious event turned all humans to stone, and uses science to rebuild civilization. A delightfully educational adventure that makes chemistry and engineering exciting.
reviews: Makes you want to study chemistry.
Senku is one of the best protagonists in modern shonen.

title: Death Note
synopsis: Genius student Light Yagami discovers a notebook that kills anyone whose name is written in it, and embarks on a crusade to become a god of a new world. A cat-and-mouse psychological thriller between two brilliant minds.
reviews: The greatest thriller manga.
L vs Kira is one of the best rivalries in fiction.

title: MONSTER
synopsis: Dr. Kenzo Tenma saves the life of a young boy who grows up to become a serial killer, and must stop the monster he created. A meticulously crafted European-set thriller exploring responsibility and the nature of evil.
reviews: Urasawa's crowning achievement.
The most novelistic manga ever created.

title: Astra Lost in Space
synopsis: A group of students on a space camping trip are suddenly transported 5,000 light-years away and must find their way home in a derelict ship. A tight, well-plotted mystery with a twist-filled narrative.
reviews: The most satisfying plot structure in recent manga.
Every detail matters and pays off.

title: The Promised Neverland
synopsis: Three orphans discover their idyllic orphanage is actually a farm that raises children as food for demons and plan their escape. A relentlessly suspenseful thriller that plays cat-and-mouse with nightmarish precision.
reviews: The best first 30 chapters in manga history.
Legitimately terrifying.

title: Liar Game
synopsis: Naïve Nao Kanzaki is drawn into the Liar Game Tournament, where players use manipulation and deception to steal each other's money. A fiendishly clever psychological thriller built around elaborate game theory scenarios.
reviews: The smartest game-theory manga.
Each arc introduces a brilliant new game design.

title: Erased
synopsis: Failed manga artist Satoru Fujinuma is sent back in time to his childhood after his mother is murdered, and must prevent past kidnappings. A tightly constructed mystery thriller with deep emotional resonance.
reviews: One of the most emotionally effective manga thrillers.
The relationship between Satoru and Kayo is heartbreaking.

title: Homunculus
synopsis: A trepanation experiment gives homeless man Nakoshi the ability to see people's inner selves as grotesque homunculi. A disturbing and fascinating psychological drama exploring trauma, identity, and perception.
reviews: Unlike anything else.
Disturbing but impossible to look away from.

title: Spiral: Suiri no Kizuna
synopsis: High school student Ayumu Narumi investigates a string of murders connected to the mysterious Blade Children. A sharp detective mystery with strong logical puzzles and an intriguing cast.
reviews: One of the smartest detective manga.
The mystery is genuinely compelling.

title: Doubt
synopsis: A group of teens playing a werewolf-style phone game are trapped in an abandoned building and start dying one by one. A claustrophobic death game thriller with genuine tension.
reviews: Short but perfectly paced.
The tension never lets up.

title: Parasyte
synopsis: Alien parasites invade Earth and consume human hosts, but one fails to reach Shinichi's brain and bonds with his hand instead. A quietly devastating horror story about humanity, empathy, and what makes us human.
reviews: One of the most philosophically interesting horror manga.
Migi is one of the best characters in manga.

title: Yotsuba&!
synopsis: Five-year-old Yotsuba explores the world around her new suburban neighborhood with insatiable curiosity and boundless enthusiasm. A masterpiece of gentle, pure comedy that captures the joy of experiencing everything for the first time.
reviews: The happiest manga in existence.
Kiyohiko Azuma captures childhood perfectly.

title: Gintama
synopsis: Gintoki Sakata, a freelance samurai with a sweet tooth, navigates Edo-period Japan after aliens have taken over and banned swords. A genre-defying comedy that can switch from absurdist parody to profound drama without warning.
reviews: The funniest manga ever written and also one of the most moving.
Sorachi is a comedic genius.

title: March Comes in Like a Lion
synopsis: Teenage professional shogi player Rei Kiriyama battles depression and isolation while finding solace in an eccentric but caring family. An extraordinarily sensitive and visually inventive portrait of mental illness and recovery.
reviews: The most emotionally mature manga.
The most honest portrayal of depression in any medium.

title: Barakamon
synopsis: Struggling calligrapher Seishuu Handa is exiled to a rural island, where the locals and one irrepressible little girl teach him what life is really about. Warm, funny, and deeply restorative.
reviews: The most comforting manga I've ever read.
Naru is the best child character in manga.

title: Silver Spoon
synopsis: City boy Yugo Hachiken enrolls in an agricultural high school to escape pressure, and learns humbling lessons about food, work, and life. An honest and funny manga by the Fullmetal Alchemist creator.
reviews: Arakawa's most underrated work.
Genuinely changed how I think about food.

title: Daily Lives of High School Boys
synopsis: Three high school boys and their classmates navigate the absurdities of adolescence with a relentless stream of surreal sketches. One of the funniest manga ever made, perfecting the art of the setup and payoff.
reviews: Funnier than most stand-up comedy.
Every chapter ends with a perfect punchline.

title: Azumanga Daioh
synopsis: A group of high school girls experience the joys and absurdities of school life in a 4-koma format. The foundational 'cute girls doing cute things' manga that defined the moe genre.
reviews: The origin point of an entire subgenre.
Still hilarious decades later.

title: Lucky Star
synopsis: Otaku Konata Izumi and her friends discuss anime, video games, and the minutiae of Japanese school life. A deeply self-referential comedy that celebrates fandom culture.
reviews: A love letter to otaku culture.
Konata is an icon.

title: K-On!
synopsis: A group of high school girls form a light music club and spend more time eating cake than practicing. An incredibly soothing and lovable slice-of-life that inspired countless imitators.
reviews: The warmest manga.
No plot, only vibes, absolutely perfect.

title: Non Non Biyori
synopsis: Children in a tiny rural Japanese village with a single combined classroom enjoy the unhurried rhythms of country life. Possibly the most tranquil manga in existence, a perfect antidote to stress.
reviews: Therapeutic.
Renge is one of the most unique child characters in manga.

title: Slam Dunk
synopsis: Delinquent Hanamichi Sakuragi joins the basketball team to impress a girl and discovers a genuine talent and passion for the sport. A legendary sports manga that introduced basketball to a generation of Japanese readers.
reviews: The greatest sports manga.
Changed basketball in Japan forever.

title: Haikyuu!!
synopsis: Short but passionate Shoyo Hinata aspires to be a volleyball ace despite his height, joining a high school team and forming an unlikely partnership with his rival setter. Masterful at capturing the physical and emotional intensity of sports.
reviews: The best modern sports manga.
Furudate makes you care about every character.

title: The Prince of Tennis
synopsis: Middle-school prodigy Ryoma Echizen joins his high school's tennis team and faces increasingly powerful opponents. An over-the-top sports fantasy where tennis moves defy physics.
reviews: Absurd but irresistible.
Defined the power-scaling sports genre.

title: Kuroko's Basketball
synopsis: The phantom sixth man of a legendary basketball team joins a new school and aims to defeat his former teammates using misdirection and teamwork. A cool, stylish sports thriller built around supernatural-level basketball skills.
reviews: The coolest sports manga aesthetic.
The Generation of Miracles concept is genius.

title: Eyeshield 21
synopsis: Timid Sena Kobayakawa is strong-armed into being the star running back for his school's American football team, hiding behind a visor. A high-energy sports comedy that makes American football irresistibly exciting.
reviews: Made me love American football.
Hiruma is one of the best characters in sports manga.

title: Hajime no Ippo
synopsis: Shy high schooler Ippo Makunouchi discovers boxing after a champion defends him from bullies and becomes determined to understand what it means to be strong. The definitive boxing manga.
reviews: The most emotionally powerful sports manga.
Still running and still incredible.

title: Captain Tsubasa
synopsis: Soccer prodigy Tsubasa Ozora dreams of winning the World Cup as Japan's ace striker, inspiring multiple generations of real-life soccer players. A foundational sports manga with superhuman technique.
reviews: Inspired countless real soccer players.
Pure childhood joy.

title: Hikaru no Go
synopsis: Middle schooler Hikaru Shindo discovers an ancient go board and is possessed by the ghost of a Heian-era master who wants to achieve the Hand of God. An astonishingly compelling story about a board game.
reviews: Impossible to put down despite being about go.
One of the best sports series ever made.

title: Yowamushi Pedal
synopsis: Anime-loving loner Sakamichi Onoda unknowingly has incredible cycling endurance and joins his school's bicycle racing club. A lovable underdog sports story that makes competitive cycling thrilling.
reviews: The most enthusiastic sports manga.
Onoda's joy is infectious.

title: Ace of Diamond
synopsis: Brash pitcher Eijun Sawamura joins a powerhouse baseball high school and competes to become its ace. A richly detailed baseball manga with a vast cast of compelling rivals.
reviews: The best baseball manga since Major.
The depth of the supporting cast is extraordinary.

title: Tokyo Ghoul
synopsis: College student Ken Kaneki survives an attack by a ghoul and is transformed into a half-ghoul, forced to navigate the violent underground of ghoul society. A visceral exploration of otherness, identity, and the monster within.
reviews: A compelling horror action series.
The first arc is a masterpiece of body horror and identity.

title: Higurashi: When They Cry
synopsis: Keiichi Maebara moves to a quiet village where his new friends may be connected to a series of mysterious disappearances and gruesome deaths. A masterfully constructed horror mystery that uses repeated timelines to devastating effect.
reviews: The most innovative horror manga structure.
Genuinely terrifying and then genuinely heartwarming.

title: Deadman Wonderland
synopsis: Ganta Igarashi is wrongly convicted and sent to a prison theme park where inmates fight for entertainment, discovering he can weaponize his own blood. A brutal sci-fi horror action series.
reviews: Wildly inventive premise.
The Branch of Sin power system is creative.

title: Elfen Lied
synopsis: Lucy, a mutant with telekinetic killing vectors, escapes a research facility and is taken in by students who are unaware of her dangerous past. An extremely violent but emotionally complex story about discrimination and trauma.
reviews: Brutal but emotionally devastating.
The diclonius concept is disturbing and fascinating.

title: Hellsing
synopsis: The Hellsing Organization, led by the aristocratic Integra and her vampire servant Alucard, battles supernatural threats to England. A stylish and over-the-top action horror series dripping with gothic excess.
reviews: Alucard is one of the greatest characters in manga.
Pure gothic action spectacle.

title: Shiki
synopsis: A wave of mysterious deaths plagues a remote Japanese village as a doctor tries to uncover the truth about an ancient evil that has awakened. A slow-burn horror that offers genuine sympathy for its monsters.
reviews: The most morally complex horror manga.
Ono Fuyumi's story is masterfully adapted.

title: Umineko: When They Cry
synopsis: The Ushiromiya family is stranded on an island as members begin dying, seemingly following the prophecy of a witch. A dense, intricate mystery that wrestles with the nature of truth and the power of fiction.
reviews: The most complex mystery in manga.
Ryukishi07's magnum opus.

title: Chainsaw Man
synopsis: Denji, a destitute boy merged with a chainsaw devil, becomes a devil hunter who just wants to eat good food and touch a girl. A subversive, darkly comedic action horror manga that refuses to follow genre conventions.
reviews: The most original manga of the 2020s.
Fujimoto deconstructs shonen with surgical precision.

title: Uzumaki
synopsis: The residents of the coastal town Kurôzu-cho become increasingly obsessed with spiral patterns as the town itself seems to descend into madness. Junji Ito's most sustained horror narrative is a study in escalating cosmic dread.
reviews: The most unsettling manga ever created.
Junji Ito at the height of his powers.

title: Tomie
synopsis: A beautiful girl named Tomie drives men to madness and murder, but cannot be killed and always regenerates from her remains. Junji Ito's debut work is a disturbing and unsettling look at obsession, beauty, and death.
reviews: An instant horror classic.
Tomie is one of the most frightening characters in fiction.

title: Vagabond
synopsis: A fictionalized account of legendary swordsman Miyamoto Musashi's journey to become invincible and the toll that path exacts on his soul. Takehiko Inoue's masterpiece features some of the most breathtaking artwork in manga history.
reviews: The most beautiful manga ever drawn.
Every page could be a painting.

title: Kingdom
synopsis: Young orphan Li Xin aspires to become the greatest general in China during the Warring States period and serves the future first emperor Qin Shi Huang. An epic historical saga of warfare, strategy, and ambition.
reviews: The best historical epic in manga.
The battle tactics are meticulously researched.

title: Lone Wolf and Cub
synopsis: Disgraced shogunate executioner Ogami Ittō walks the 'demon way' of vengeance with his infant son in a wooden cart. A foundational samurai manga with an astonishing cinematic visual language.
reviews: The most cinematic manga.
Influenced everything from Westerns to modern samurai stories.

title: Rurouni Kenshin
synopsis: Legendary assassin Kenshin Himura wanders Meiji Japan as a pacifist swordsman atoning for his violent past, bearing a reverse-blade sword. A beautifully balanced historical action story about redemption and the weight of the past.
reviews: The defining Meiji-era manga.
The Kyoto arc is one of the best story arcs in manga.

title: Golden Kamuy
synopsis: Post-Russo-Japanese War veteran Sugimoto teams up with an Ainu girl to decode tattooed maps leading to a massive gold stash. A wild and unpredictable historical adventure packed with survival skills, authentic cultural detail, and outrageous humor.
reviews: The most entertaining historical manga.
A feast of food, culture, and absurdist humor.

title: Vinland Saga
synopsis: Young Thorfinn seeks revenge against the mercenary Askeladd who killed his father, while gradually learning the futility of violence. A stunning Viking epic that evolves from action revenge story to profound anti-war statement.
reviews: The most profound anti-war manga.
Askeladd is one of the greatest characters in manga.

title: Spy x Family
synopsis: Master spy Loid Forger assembles a fake family, including a telepath child and an assassin wife, to complete an undercover mission. A brilliantly structured comedy of errors built on the sweetest found-family dynamic in recent manga.
reviews: The feel-good manga of the decade.
Anya is the most lovable character in recent memory.

title: Maus
synopsis: Art Spiegelman documents his father's survival of the Holocaust using anthropomorphic animals, with Jews as mice and Nazis as cats. A Pulitzer Prize-winning masterwork of comics journalism that changed how the medium was perceived.
reviews: The most important comic ever made.
Required reading.

title: Watchmen
synopsis: Retired superheroes investigate a murder in an alternate 1985 America where the existence of a genuine superman has changed geopolitics. Alan Moore's deconstruction of superhero mythology remains the genre's most profound work.
reviews: The greatest graphic novel.
Nothing has matched it as a superhero deconstruction.

title: Saga
synopsis: Two soldiers from opposite sides of a galactic war fall in love and must protect their newborn daughter from countless enemies across the universe. Brian K. Vaughan and Fiona Staples' genre-defying space opera is one of the best comics of the modern era.
reviews: The most imaginative sci-fi comic being published.
Staples' art is extraordinary.
